action: Understand the scope and application of the manual
acceptance_criterion: Operator demonstrates understanding of the two procedures, the 11-component state model, and the notation system used in the manual.
deviation_patterns: Failure to distinguish between Procedure A and Procedure B
Misinterpretation of the 11-component state model
Confusion regarding the notation system (orange, red, blue, green, magenta)

action: Understand the tools and fastening method.
acceptance_criterion: Operator must confirm that all fastening is performed by hand using push-fit pins and finger-tightened screws and acorn nuts, without any tools.
deviation_patterns: Using pliers, drivers, or other tools.
Applying excessive torque that cracks printed PLA bosses.

action: Sort all parts by color and verify completeness against the chapter parts list.
acceptance_criterion: All parts are sorted by color and verified to be complete and undamaged.
deviation_patterns: Parts are not sorted by color.
Parts are missing or incomplete.
Damaged or warped parts are not rejected.

action: Verify the type and length of every fastener against the instruction for that step before installation.
acceptance_criterion: Each fastener matches the specified type and length in the instruction for the step.
deviation_patterns: Installation of a wrong pin type.
Substitution of an acorn nut for the bracket screw.

action: Compare the build to the verification strip at every checkpoint.
acceptance_criterion: Each component's installation status matches the corresponding box on the verification strip (orange 1 for installed, white 0 for not installed).
deviation_patterns: Mismatch between build and verification strip
Failure to stop work upon detecting a nonconformance

action: Understand execution-order policy
acceptance_criterion: Operator understands that Chapters 2 and 3 may be executed in either order, pin order may be interchangeable where stated, and all checkpoints must pass before work continues.
deviation_patterns: Skipping checkpoints before continuing work.
Considering a step complete without correct installation and tightening of components.
Misinterpreting interchangeable pin order or execution sequence flexibility.

action: Remove both hands from the assembly before verification.
acceptance_criterion: The operator must ensure an unobstructed view of the build state for verification.
deviation_patterns: Hands remain on the assembly during verification.
Verification performed over an unverified or suspect step.

action: Fabricate replacement parts using specified parameters.
acceptance_criterion: Verify that all 36 parts are fabricated with 0.3 mm layer height, 15% infill, 50 mm/s speed, raft build-plate adhesion, and 0.5 mm hole horizontal expansion.
deviation_patterns: Layer height is not set to 0.3 mm.
Infill percentage is not set to 15%.
Printing speed is not set to 50 mm/s.
Build-plate adhesion is not set to raft.
Hole horizontal expansion is not set to 0.5 mm.

action: Select the applicable procedure from the work order
acceptance_criterion: The operator must correctly identify and select the procedure based on the work order and verify the unit state if Procedure B is chosen.
deviation_patterns: Incorrect procedure selected from the work order.
Failure to verify incoming unit state for Procedure B.
Proceeding with Procedure B without dispositioning a unit not at state 13.

action: Verify parts required for this chapter.
acceptance_criterion: Operator confirms the presence of all required parts and verifies the pin type and length against the work instruction.
deviation_patterns: Missing required parts
Incorrect pin type or length used

action: Place the base on a flat surface in the standard orientation and inspect for damage or warping.
acceptance_criterion: The base is placed on a flat surface in the standard orientation and is free of damage or warping.
deviation_patterns: Base is not placed on a flat surface.
Base is not in the standard orientation.
Base shows signs of damage or warping.

action: Install the front chassis onto the base
acceptance_criterion: The front chassis must be fully seated with no gap at the joint, bores aligned, and squareness verified.
deviation_patterns: Misaligned front chassis
Gap at the joint
Bores not aligned
Squareness not verified

action: Install the front chassis pin
acceptance_criterion: The pin must be of the correct type, correct length, silver, fully seated through aligned bores, and have the correct head orientation as per the work instruction.
deviation_patterns: Wrong pin type
Reversed pin orientation

action: Verify front chassis assembly completion.
acceptance_criterion: Front chassis is flush, fully engaged, correctly oriented, cannot rotate or lift from the base, and matches the specified build configuration.
deviation_patterns: Front chassis not flush or fully engaged.
Incorrect type or orientation of front pin.
Front chassis rotates or lifts from the base.
Hands obstructing the view during verification.
Build does not match the specified configuration.

action: Verify parts required for rear chassis assembly.
acceptance_criterion: Ensure the operator uses long braces and correct chassis pins as specified, and verifies brace lengths if in doubt.
deviation_patterns: Using short braces instead of long braces.
Using incorrect chassis pins.
Failing to compare brace lengths when uncertain.

action: Install the rear chassis by aligning and fitting the long braces.
acceptance_criterion: The long braces must sit parallel and their bores must be concentric with the base bores.
deviation_patterns: Braces are not parallel.
Bores are not aligned concentrically.
Braces are improperly fitted to the rear interface.

action: Install the front-rear chassis pin
acceptance_criterion: The pin is fully seated in the forward bore position of the rear chassis with correct orientation.
deviation_patterns: Pin is not fully seated.
Pin is inserted in the incorrect bore position.
Pin orientation is incorrect.

action: Install the rear-rear chassis pin
acceptance_criterion: The pin must be fully seated in the rearmost bore position with correct orientation and type.
deviation_patterns: wrong pin type
reversed pin orientation
pin not fully seated

action: Verify rear chassis assembly and alignment.
acceptance_criterion: Both pins must be fully seated, of correct type and orientation; rear chassis must be secure with no play and braces parallel; build must match the specified strip configuration.
deviation_patterns: Pins not fully seated or incorrect type/orientation.
Rear chassis has play or braces are not parallel.
Build does not match the specified strip configuration.

action: Verify parts required for this chapter.
components_required: Front bracket (1x) - white, with magenta wing per configuration
Front bracket screw (1x) - threaded shank with head
acceptance_criterion: Operator confirms the presence of the correct front bracket and front bracket screw, ensuring the screw is positively identified and not substituted with acorn nuts.
deviation_patterns: Using acorn nuts instead of the front bracket screw.
Proceeding without completing Chapters 2 and 3.

action: Mount the front bracket
acceptance_criterion: The front bracket must be aligned square to the vehicle centerline with bores properly aligned.
deviation_patterns: Bracket not square to the vehicle centerline
Bores misaligned with the chassis interface

action: Install the front bracket screw by hand and tighten to firm finger torque.
acceptance_criterion: The screw is fully threaded, head seated flush, and confirmed to be the correct screw by head shape.
deviation_patterns: Screw not fully threaded or head not seated flush.
Incorrect fastener installed, such as an acorn nut instead of the screw.

action: Verify parts required for wheel assembly chapter
acceptance_criterion: Operator must confirm all listed parts are present, with special attention to counting washers to avoid omission.
deviation_patterns: Failure to count washers before starting
Omission of wheel washers during assembly

action: Install the front wheel assembly
acceptance_criterion: Both wheels rotate freely with no axial play after assembly.
deviation_patterns: Incorrect stack order of components on the front axle.
Acorn nuts not tightened to firm finger torque.
Wheels do not rotate freely or exhibit axial play.

action: Install the rear wheel assembly
acceptance_criterion: The rear pulley is installed before the wheels, and both wheels rotate freely with the pulley present and seated.
deviation_patterns: Rear pulley missing after wheel installation
Rear pulley not seated properly
Wheels do not rotate freely
Incorrect assembly order of components

action: Verify wheel assembly components and installation.
acceptance_criterion: All washers, acorn nuts, rear pulley, and specified components are present, properly seated, and tightened to firm finger torque; assembly matches the reference strip if Chapter 4 is complete.
deviation_patterns: Missing washer behind any wheel.
Acorn nuts not tightened to firm finger torque.
Rear pulley not present or improperly seated.
Hands obstructing the view during verification.
Assembly does not match the reference strip.

action: Verify the complete vehicle state component by component against the specified configuration.
acceptance_criterion: All components must be at state 1 except the short rear chassis, which must remain at state 0, resulting in state 13 for complete assembly.
deviation_patterns: Any component not matching the specified state configuration.
Short rear chassis set to state 1 instead of state 0.

action: Perform fastener and hardware audit
acceptance_criterion: All fasteners and hardware are correctly installed, tightened, oriented, and functional as specified.
deviation_patterns: Front bracket secured by a nut instead of a screw or not fully tightened
Incorrect type, orientation, or seating of any of the four pins
Loose acorn nuts, missing washers behind wheels, missing rear pulley, or missing wing
Wheels do not rotate freely or components exhibit play

action: Record the build details and determine disposition.
acceptance_criterion: The build record must include executed step order, checkpoint results, and the final state vector; disposition must be correctly determined as PASS or FAIL.
deviation_patterns: Failure to record all required build details.
Incorrect determination of PASS or FAIL disposition.
Releasing a vehicle marked as FAIL without quarantine.

action: Verify parts and prerequisites before starting the procedure.
acceptance_criterion: Ensure all required parts are present and the vehicle is verified at state 13 before beginning work.
deviation_patterns: Missing short braces or reuse tray.
Vehicle not verified at state 13.
Front-end fasteners loosened unnecessarily.

action: Remove the rear wheel assembly and organize hardware in the reuse tray.
acceptance_criterion: The reuse tray contains exactly 2 wheels, 2 washers, 2 nuts, and 1 pulley, segregated from surplus stock.
deviation_patterns: Failure to unscrew acorn nuts by hand.
Mixing reuse hardware with station surplus stock.
Incorrect count of components in the reuse tray.

action: Remove both rear chassis pins
acceptance_criterion: Both pins are withdrawn axially and placed in the reuse tray, with bores clear and undamaged.
deviation_patterns: Pins are levered against the printed bores.
Pins are not withdrawn axially.
Pins are not placed in the reuse tray.
Bores are damaged or obstructed.

action: Remove the long rear chassis and tag it as 'removed - long configuration'.
acceptance_criterion: The rear end is stripped, the front end remains intact, and the removed long braces are tagged and routed per the work order without entering the reuse tray.
deviation_patterns: Long braces are not tagged correctly.
Long braces are placed in the reuse tray.
Front end is disturbed during removal.
Rear end does not match the stripped configuration.

action: Install the short rear chassis
acceptance_criterion: Short braces must be aligned parallel with bores concentric at the rear interface, and verified to be shorter than the removed long brace.
deviation_patterns: Using a long brace instead of a short brace.
Misalignment of braces causing non-parallel positioning.
Bores not concentric during installation.

action: Reinstall both rear chassis pins
acceptance_criterion: Both pins must be of the correct type and orientation, fully seated, and the chassis must be secure with no play.
deviation_patterns: Incorrect pin type used
Pins not fully seated
Pins installed with incorrect orientation
Chassis has play after installation

action: Reinstall the rear wheel assembly using components from the reuse tray.
acceptance_criterion: Pulley, washers, and nuts are installed; wheels rotate freely; reuse tray is empty.
deviation_patterns: Hardware missing from the vehicle
Reuse tray not empty
Wheels do not rotate freely
Nuts not tightened to firm finger torque

action: Perform final inspection of the rear-chassis upgrade.
acceptance_criterion: The vehicle matches state 22 with the chassis positions exchanged, short braces installed, no long-configuration hardware remaining, and the front end undisturbed and tight.
deviation_patterns: Chassis positions not exchanged correctly.
Long-configuration hardware still present.
Front end disturbed or loose.
Incorrect recording of job details or checkpoint results.

action: Follow the sanctioned removal step and rework sequence.
acceptance_criterion: Rework is performed by removing, verifying, and reinstalling correctly without adjustment in place, forcing, or bending.
deviation_patterns: Skipping the removal step.
Adjusting, forcing, or bending components in place.
Failing to undo concealed defects in reverse build order.

action: Understand documented error modes and corrective actions.
acceptance_criterion: Operator must be able to identify error modes and apply the prescribed corrective actions accurately.
deviation_patterns: Failure to remove affected wheel assembly when washer is omitted.
Failure to repeat Chapter 6 audit after installing omitted wheel.
Incorrect handling of wrong or reversed pin, such as not quarantining it.
Failure to quarantine acorn nut or repeat Chapter 4 hold point after replacing it with bracket screw.
Skipping rear pulley installation or Chapter 5 rebuild steps.
Incorrect handling of wrong braces, such as not stripping rear end per Chapter 7 steps.
Failure to establish true build state or skipping checkpoints when correcting omitted steps discovered late.

action: Understand the basis for the verification regime.
acceptance_criterion: Operator must acknowledge that the verification regime is based on documented failures and includes mandatory checkpoints, hold points, and sign-offs.
deviation_patterns: Failure to understand the importance of the verification regime.
Ignoring mandatory checkpoints, hold points, or sign-offs.
